title: Calculus I
prerequisites: None. Students should have completed pre-calculus or equivalent.
description: The first course in the calculus sequence covering limits, continuity, differentiation, and introduction to integration. Students study the concept of a limit, techniques of differentiation including the power rule, product rule, quotient rule, and chain rule, and applications of derivatives including optimization, related rates, and curve sketching. The course introduces the definite integral through Riemann sums and covers the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus. Applications include area under curves, average value, and introductory differential equations. This course is required for CS students as it provides the mathematical foundation for algorithm analysis, machine learning, and scientific computing. Students should have a strong background in algebra and trigonometry. A graphing calculator is required.
outcomes: Evaluate limits using algebraic and graphical methods
Apply derivatives to optimization and related rates problems
Apply the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
Model real-world problems using calculus concepts
